items.subject: ‘The cost of compute is far beyond the costs of the employee’: Nvidia executive says right now AI is more expensive than paying human workers
items.body: Big Tech has announced $740 billion in capex this year, but AI has yet to show evidence of widespread increased productivity.

items.subject: Introducing the OpenAI Partner Network
items.body: OpenAI launches the Partner Network, investing $150M to help global partners accelerate enterprise AI adoption, deployment, and transformation.

items.subject: New OpenAI Academy courses for the next era of work
items.body: OpenAI introduces three Academy courses that help people build practical AI skills, create repeatable workflows, and apply agents in everyday work.

items.subject: How Preply combines AI and human tutors to personalize learning
items.body: Preply uses OpenAI to launch AI-generated lesson summaries, providing personalised feedback and language learning exercises.

items.subject: BBVA puts AI at the core of banking with OpenAI
items.body: Learn how BBVA scaled ChatGPT Enterprise to 100,000 employees and partnered with OpenAI to accelerate AI-powered banking transformation worldwide.

items.subject: OpenAI to acquire Ona
items.body: OpenAI plans to acquire Ona to expand Codex with secure, persistent cloud environments, enabling long-running AI agents across enterprise workflows.

items.subject: NVIDIA Stockholder Meeting Set for June 24; Individuals Can Participate Online

items.subject: Supporting Europe’s work in ensuring a trustworthy AI ecosystem
items.body: OpenAI supports the EU Code of Practice on AI content transparency, advancing provenance standards and tools to help people understand AI-generated content.

items.subject: How an astrophysicist uses Codex to help simulate black holes
items.body: Discover how astrophysicist Chi-kwan Chan uses Codex to build black hole simulations, helping scientists study extreme physics and test Einstein’s theory of general relativity.

items.subject: PRC-linked influence operations are targeting AI debates in the US
items.body: A new report from OpenAI details PRC-linked influence operations using AI to target U. S. tech debates, data center narratives, tariffs, and false claims about ChatGPT.

summary:
Here are the key developments with insight:

**4-6 Bullet Points:**

* **OpenAI Launches Partner Network:** OpenAI has launched its Partner Network, investing $150M to help global partners accelerate enterprise AI adoption, deployment, and transformation.
* **AI Cost Concerns:** ARR researcher Mike Grove estimates that AI costs are currently higher than paying human workers, casting doubts on the productivity gains often expected from AI adoption.
* **NVIDIA Acquires Techniques to Enhance Codex:** OpenAI plans to acquire Ona to expand Codex with secure, persistent cloud environments, enabling long-running AI agents across enterprise workflows.
* **BBVA Leverages OpenAI:** BBVA has scaled ChatGPT Enterprise to 100,000 employees and partnered with OpenAI to accelerate AI-powered banking transformation worldwide.
* **OpenAI Supports Transparency in AI Ecosystem:** OpenAI has joined the EU Code of Practice on AI content transparency, advancing provenance standards and tools to help people understand AI-generated content.
* **AI Applications in Astrophysics:** Astrophysicist Chi-kwan Chan uses Codex to build black hole simulations, helping scientists study extreme physics and test Einstein's theory of general relativity.

**Trend:**

* **Growing Focus on Responsible AI Development:** The tech industry is shifting its focus towards developing AI responsibly, with OpenAI's support for the EU Code of Practice on AI content transparency and NVIDIA's involvement in ensuring a trustworthy AI ecosystem demonstrating this trend.
